action.reasoning: Observation: Training failed for 20+ iterations with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'xgboost' (packages_to_install was never included in prior JSONs despite code in model.py/train.py/predict.py being fully ready for XGBRegressor on Morgan FP r=3/2048bit, objective=reg:absoluteerror, hist method, n_est=1000, lr=0.05, EarlyStopping callback on val, metrics logging to RAB_ITERATION dir, best.pkl save/load). Best MAE remains 0.2188 from RF (iter6); MLP was worse (0.231). Iter7 curve shows good convergence but model limited. Analysis: xgboost is explicitly allowed and will persist after install; with MAE objective + early_stop on val FP data it should beat RF baseline (~0.15-0.18 MAE expected) on this high-dim sparse tabular QM9 task. FP calc (~4-6min) fits in 1.77h/3 iters left. No code bugs (current train.py has valid callback without save_best=True). Decision: Install xgboost now, run current train.py on full CSV data (trains with callback+eval_set, logs val_mae, saves best.pkl), submit_eval on best.pkl for test MAE improvement + timing before Mordred/3D GNN exploration.
